channel.title: Posts on Yahoon's Tech & Tales
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Yahoon's Tech & Tales

item.title: Install Helm Chart from tgz File
item.description:
Originally posted on yahoon.github.io. This is original content; please provide a backlink to the original post if you wish to cross-post.

Offline Installation of Helm Chart (tar.gz File)

Recently I’ve been learning Helm. As an operations engineer, I haven’t had much exposure to deploying applications on K8s, so I decided to get some hands-on practice.

Using the official podinfo chart, the steps are as follows:

Step 1: Pull the Chart

The chart package itself is a tar.gz archive.

item.title: Setting Up Your Personal Password Manager: VaultWarden
item.description:
Installing the Password Management Server VaultWarden

Background

I’ve been using Bitwarden as my password manager, but some issues have gradually emerged:

  • Access from China is always slow, probably because the servers are overseas
  • What if one day it gets blocked?

After some comparison and research, I chose to self-host VaultWarden

Why VaultWarden?

  • Cost of self-hosting Bitwarden: Although the official Bitwarden supports self-hosting, its backend is written in .NET and is very memory-hungry (typically requires 2GB~4GB+ RAM), which is unfriendly for my PC. Vaultwarden, on the other hand, is extremely lightweight, requiring only tens of MB of RAM to run smoothly.
  • Compatibility: Vaultwarden is fully compatible with Bitwarden’s official apps and browser extensions. You just need to fill in your self-hosted URL in the “Self-hosted URL” field when logging in on the client side.
  • Deployment: Vaultwarden supports one-click deployment with Podman / Docker, while Bitwarden requires Docker Compose.

Introduction

Project page: https://github.com/dani-garcia/vaultwarden

item.title: Create Root CA for Home Lab
item.description:
Background

Many services on my local network run directly over IP, which poses a security threat that cannot be ignored. In an isolated environment, it is important to set up a local Root CA for the LAN.

The tool used is very simple => mkcert

About mkcert

mkcert is a zero-configuration local CA solution.

Open source project: https://github.com/FiloSottile/mkcert

Environment

Use my desktop as the hosting server, it is Ubuntu 26.04, IP: 192.168.1.4

item.title: Semaphore UI Quick Install
item.description:
Background

I’m a long-time user of Ansible Automation Platform (AAP), and I deeply understand the importance of an automation platform. As the upstream of AAP, the open-source project AWX hasn’t been updated for a while, with the latest version still stuck in 2024.

This forced me to look for alternative tools.

Through an AI search, Semaphore UI stood out. It’s an AAP-like automation operations platform with a Web UI that supports not only Ansible but also Terraform and others.

item.title: Mysterious Machine Intercommunication Failure in Home Lab
item.description:
Introduction

In my home lab, I have two Linux machines:

  • Ubuntu 26.04 (IP: 192.168.1.4): acts as my test server, running my PoC environment
  • Fedora 44 (IP: 192.168.1.142): acts as my workstation

They have been working perfectly well (I hate the waiting time for booting, logging in, and reopening applications, so I rarely shut them down). Every day I SSH from my workstation to the Ubuntu server for various tests, until this Monday.

item.title: Thoughts after an Interview
item.description:
The Whole Story

I’ve attended several interviews recently, one of which sparked some deep self-reflection.

Had an interview yesterday, and the interviewer asked me:

“What have you been up to lately?”
“Organizing my past.”
“Is there really that much to organize?”
“Nearly twenty years of career accumulation—how could there not be?”

Even looking just at my last 12+ years at Red Hat, I’m still far from finishing sorting through everything I’ve gained.

item.title: Setup Container Registry for Home Lab
item.description:
Introduction

I’m building my home lab. In corporate environments, various services are ready to use out of the box, so I’ve never had to worry about services being blocked or slow network speeds.

However, in a home lab network environment, many websites are inaccessible, or it’s hard to find free alternatives. The most typical example is Docker Hub. So I had to set up my own.

My requirements are simple:

  • Only for use within the home lab intranet
  • Any machine on the intranet can push and pull images
  • HTTP access is sufficient (no HTTPS needed)
  • No authentication required

Server

My desktop:

item.title: Install Dictionary on Fedora
item.description:
Background

As part of the starting a new journey, I had to reconfigure my personal laptop. Everything had to start from scratch — from the ISO image, to system installation, to configuration, to being fully functional. It wasn’t as easy as it seemed.

Especially when I realized that, even for some of the most common tools, I had to learn and install from scratch (or maybe I just hadn’t paid enough attention before).

item.title: Xiaojiu - My Cat
item.description:
Introducing My Kitty - Xiaojiu

Who is he

Name: Xiaojiu

Birth: February 2022

Gender: Male

Personality: Lazy, sometimes gentle, sometimes grumpy, not very talkative

When the story started

It was a stray cat. On March 3, 2022, just one month old, it crawled into my car. From then on, it officially became a member of our family.

Its name comes from this special day—March 3rd. Unconsciously, it reminded me of “San San De Jiu (三三得九)” from the Chinese multiplication table《九九乘法表》.

item.title: Install WSL on Windows 11
item.description:
Intro

WSL: Windows Subsystem for Linux

WSL is a tiny Linux VM embedded in Windows, default is Ubuntu.

Homepage: https://github.com/microsoft/WSL

One-Click Install (Online Install)

wsl --install

Issue: wsl --install is very slow, mainly because Microsoft’s overseas servers have poor bandwidth in China. Fastest solution: Offline manual install (domestic mirror download), done in a few minutes.

Manual Install (Offline Install)

Prerequisites

Windows 11 Home fully supports WSL2, no Hyper-V required. Just enable two system features, install the MSI you downloaded, and import the wsl image - works fully offline.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Bio

Yahoon Yao

Engineer, Open Source Advocate, Tech Writer, Lifelong Learner

An IT veteran with 20+ years of experience across Linux, Cloud Computing, AWS, Ansible, Infrastructure Operations and DevOps fields.

Some keywords about me: Open Source | Linux | SRE | Cloud Computing | AWS | GCP | Terraform | Golden VM image builder | DevOps | CI/CD | Kubernetes | Python | Go | AI coding | Monitoring

Details

  • 20+ years of enterprise-level operations and management experience, specializing in infrastructure and application operations. Expert in full-chain operations management from process standardization to implementation, with solid theoretical foundation and extensive practical experience.
